title: Generating Pod Yamls With All Containers
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm trying to put multiple things in the same pod now, so I can setup everything from one yaml file. But somehow I don't get all the containers printed. I've built a simple scenario to reproduce it:

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ git clone https://github.com/rhdojun/simpleserver.git
$ cd simpleserver
$ sudo podman pod create --name simpleserver -p 8080:80 -p 9117:9117
$ sudo podman run --pod=simpleserver --health-cmd 'curl http://localhost || exit 1' --health-interval=30s --name simpleserver1 --detach simpleserver:latest
$ sudo /usr/bin/podman run --pod=simpleserver --name apache-sidecar -detach bitnami/apache-exporter
# both pods are running
$ sudo podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS                                         NAMES
cd68eada8e95  docker.io/bitnami/apache-exporter:latest                        4 minutes ago  Up 4 minutes ago  0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:9117->9117/tcp  apache-sidecar
eff78112473c  localhost/simpleserver:latest             /usr/sbin/httpd -...  5 minutes ago  Up 5 minutes ago  0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:9117->9117/tcp  simpleserver1

# but if I generate the yaml for the pod I don't see both containers
# first of all it doesn't work with sudo for some reason
$  sudo podman generate kube simpleserver
Error: no matching entries in passwd file
$  podman generate kube simpleserver
# Generation of Kubernetes YAML is still under development!
#
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-1.6.4
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2020-07-31T15:06:56Z"
  labels:
    app: simpleserver
  name: simpleserver
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - /usr/sbin/httpd
    - -D
    - FOREGROUND
    env:
    - name: PATH
      value: /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
    - name: TERM
      value: xterm
    - name: HOSTNAME
    - name: container
      value: podman
    image: localhost/simpleserver:latest
    name: simpleserver
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
      hostPort: 8080
      protocol: TCP
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
      capabilities: {}
      privileged: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
    workingDir: /
status: {}
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Pod yaml contains one container but not the other.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Pod yaml describes both containers in a way that they could be started from that yaml.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

host:

  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.2"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 106325
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 231072
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 106325
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 231072
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 11700727808
  MemTotal: 33395630080
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc10.module+el8.2.0+5762+aaee29fb.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 14853263360
  SwapTotal: 16852709376

  cpus: 8

  kernel: 4.18.0-193.el8.x86_64

    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.2+dev
      commit: 21fdece2737dc24ffa3f01a341b8a6854f8b13b4
  uptime: 270h 5m 3.26s (Approximately 11.25 days)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

CSB

comments.body:
The `Error: no matching entries in passwd file` error messages in the output of `sudo podman generate kube simpleserver` is probably relevant.

I wouldn't expect data from `podman generate kube simpleserver`, which appears to have been run as an unprivileged user, to "see" pods and containers created as root using `sudo podman`.  The ports list in the pasted YAML is also missing an entry for 9117.  It looks like the unprivileged user is also running a pod named `simpleserver`, but with different containers in it.

Can you re-run `sudo podman generate kube simpleserver`, but with more logging, as in `sudo podman --log-level=debug generate kube simpleserver`, to see if the additional information provides any clues?
@rhdojun Could you start from the begining and run all commands as root or as non root and then see if this situation arises?

Thanks for your feedback!

@rhatdan Running everything without sudo didn't work, but currently can't remember why. But everything in sudo is there, it just errored out in the last step for some reason.

What I did since the creation of the ticket is build my own podman from master. So I could try it again with the current master and both in sudo and non-sudo cases it recognizes two containers. Is that good enough for a close?

If you want to continue testing let me know. I think there should also be a way to reset my package manager to put in the old binaries again.
Sounds like it is fixed to me.  We can reopen if you find more problems.
